comments.text:
in opencode 1.4.4 it no longer spawns or depend on ripgrep

we've integrated it natively into opencode thanks to

's wonderful package

this is a first step before we move fully over to fff for all our search operations so that things work well on huge codebases

comments.author: Matt Pocock
comments.text:
I've found myself writing:

"I don't know this area of code well. Go up a layer of abstraction. Give me a map of all the relevant modules and callers."

Might need a new skill here. What should I name it?

comments.author: Mitchell Hashimoto
comments.text: One of the most requested GitHub features in years and the website looks like it was designed by someone 9 years into a 2 year community college program.

author: Matt Pocock
text: Throwback to when I used to be good at Figma

comments.author: Matt Pocock
comments.text:
By far the strangest TypeScript trick I've ever come across is the Loose Autocomplete trick.

No idea who discovered this, but it's become widely used by OSS wizards.

It's now even used by React's types to type attributes like aria-role.

comments.author: Matt Pocock
comments.text:
I don't _really_ know why this works, but here's my best guess:

Without the & {}, TypeScript collapses 'small' | 'large' | string into just 'string'. It does this IMMEDIATELY. If you hover over the first example of Size, you'll see that it's just 'string'.

comments.author: Matt Pocock
comments.text:
But WITH the & {}, it doesn't collapse the union immediately. It does later (when it's used).

If you hover the second Size type, you'll see it's still 'small' | 'large' | (string & {}), not 'string'.

comments.author: Matt Pocock
comments.text: So that means there's a window that the union hasn't been collapsed where autocomplete can be provided.

comments.author: Matt Pocock
comments.text:
The earliest sighting I've seen is a suggestion in a thread by 
@SeaRyanC

comments.author: Carlos Zanella
comments.text:
What's your opinion on this kind of thing?

I'm very against this type of trick, because you're making your code confusing and not adding much value out of it.

Id I see "'something' | string" during a code review, my first instinct is to reject it because it surely looks wrong.

comments.author: Matt Pocock
Ken Tan
comments.text: I think type Size on the first LOC should be a type SizeOrAnything

comments.author: Matt Pocock
comments.text: Whoops, my mistake!

comments.author: create_react_app
comments.text: Does the {} mean you can pass in objects too?

comments.author: Matt Pocock
comments.text: Nope! Give it a go!

comments.author: Ian Simmons
comments.text: Stuff like this (undocumented) seems like unintended happy side affect? Makes me wonder if I use it now will it break in future version of TS? For TS tricks/hacks in general

comments.author: Matt Pocock
comments.text: TS tests itself against DefinitelyTyped - a MASSIVE collection of library types, so stuff like this never breaks unless planned.

comments.text: Where does the type SizeOrAnyString come from?
